zh.Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England: 英國戴文托爾巴易布利哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Cole I, Knight of Agincourt於大約1384年出生在英國戴文托爾巴易布利哈姆時，他的父親William Cole Esq.30歲，母親Margarete Beaupell24歲。他於大約1408年與Lady Agnes FitzWarin在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1473年在英國戴文享年91歲。

zh-hans.Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England: 英国戴文托爾巴易布利哈姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Cole I, Knight of Agincourt于大约1384年出生在英国戴文托爾巴易布利哈姆时，他的父亲William Cole Esq.30岁，母亲Margarete Beaupell24岁。他于大约1408年与Lady Agnes FitzWarin在英国戴文结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1473年在英国戴文享年91岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), Coll(e), Coul(e), a pet form of Nicol (see Nichol and Nicholas ), a common personal name from the mid 13th century onward. English families with this name migrated to Scotland and to Ulster (especially Fermanagh).

English: occasionally perhaps from a different (early) Middle English personal name Col, of native English or Scandinavian origin. Old English Cola was originally a nickname from Old English col ‘coal’ in the sense ‘coal-black (of hair), swarthy’ and is the probable source of most of the examples in Domesday Book. In the northern and eastern counties of England settled by Vikings in the 10th and 11th centuries, alternative sources are Old Norse Kolr and Koli (either from a nickname ‘the swarthy one’ or a short form of names in Kol-), and Old Norse Kollr (from a nickname, perhaps ‘the bald one’).
English: nickname for someone with swarthy skin or black hair, from Middle English col, coul(e) ‘charcoal, coal’ (Old English col).

variants.name: Colton
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Nottinghamshire): habitational name from any of various places called Colton in England. Examples in Norfolk, Staffordshire, and North Yorkshire are from the Old English personal name Cola (or the cognate Old Norse Koli; see Cole 2) + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. The place so named in Somerset has as its first element the Old English personal name Cūla (of uncertain origin). The one in Cumbria has a river name apparently derived from a Celtic word meaning ‘hazel’. This English name is also common in Ireland; it was the name of a bishop of Derry in 1397. There seems also to have been confusion with Culliton .
Scottish and Irish (Monaghan): shortened and altered form of Gaelic Mac Haldan, ‘son of Haldan’, see Haldane .

variants.name: Colclough
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Cheshire): habitational name from Cowclough in the parish of Whitworth, Lancashire, earlier recorded as Colleclogh, probably named with the Old English byname Cola (see Cole 2) + Old English clōh ‘ravine’.

variants.name: Calcote
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in Bedfordshire, Berkshire, Cambridgeshire, Cheshire, Northamptonshire, Warwickshire, and elsewhere) named Caldecote or Caldecott, from Old English cald ‘cold’ + cot ‘cottage, dwelling’. It has been suggested that in Old English this expression denoted an unattended shelter for wayfarers, although in fact some places with this name were of considerable status by 1086, when they appear in Domesday Book. In some instances this and some of the other shortened forms may have arisen from Calcot in Berkshire, Collacott(s) in Devon, or Calcutt in Wiltshire, in all of which the first element apparently comes from the Old English personal name Cola (see Cole 2) or the word col ‘(char)coal’, in which case the meaning would be something like ‘coalshed’.

variants.name: Collignon
meanings: French and Walloon: from a shortened pet form (see Cole and Colle ) of the French personal name Nicolas (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Colling
meanings: English (northern England and Devon): from the Old Norse personal name Kollungr, a derivative of Koli, or from Old English Colling, a derivative of Cola (see Cole ). Alternatively, from a pet form of the personal name Col(l), a pet form of Nicholas (see also Colin ).
German: variant of   Kolling   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Collard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Donat, Normand, Octave, Adelard, Aldor, Andre, Armand, Emile, Germaine, Marcel.
English (southern), French, and Walloon: from a diminutive of the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), a pet form of Nic(h)olas (see Cole ), formed with the Old French suffix -ard.

variants.name: Coale
meanings: English: post-medieval form of   Cole  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Coles
meanings: English (southern): variant of Cole , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Nichol
meanings: Scottish and northern English: from the Middle English personal name Nic(h)ol(e), a vernacular form of Nic(h)olas (see Nicholas ). Compare Nickel and Nicol .

In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Greek Nikolas , its variant Nicolas , or of any of various derivatives of the personal name Nikola(o)s ‘Nicholas’, including Nikolis (see Nicoli ). Compare Nicholas and Nicol .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Cole I, Knight of Agincourt was born about 1384, in Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England, his father, William Cole Esq., was 30 and his mother, Margarete Beaupell, was 24. He married Lady Agnes FitzWarin about 1408, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1473, in Devon, England, at the age of 91.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Cole I, Knight of Agincourt est né environ 1384, à Brixham, Torbay, Devon, Angleterre, son père, William Cole Esq., avait 30 ans et sa mère, Margarete Beaupell, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Lady Agnes FitzWarin environ 1408, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1473, à Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 91 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Cole I, Knight of Agincourt um 1384 in Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Cole Esq. 30 und seine Mutter Margarete Beaupell 24. Er hat um 1408 in Devon, England Lady Agnes FitzWarin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1473 im Alter von 91 Jahren in Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Cole I, Knight of Agincourt è nato il circa 1384 nel Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England, il suo padre, William Cole Esq., aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Margarete Beaupell, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Lady Agnes FitzWarin circa 1408, nel Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1473, nel Devon, England, all'età di 91 anni.

ja.Nythway Devon England: イギリスイギリスデボン
ja.Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England: イギリスデボントルバイブリクスハム
ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Cole I, Knight of Agincourtが約1384年にイギリスデボントルバイブリクスハムで生まれた時，父William Cole Esq.は30歳，母Margarete Beaupellは24歳であった。約1408年にイギリスデボンでLady Agnes FitzWarinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1473年にイギリスデボンで91歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Cole I, Knight of Agincourt(이)가 대략 1384년 영국 데폰 토르베이 브리스함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Cole Esq.(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Margarete Beaupell(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1408년 영국 데폰에서 Lady Agnes FitzWarin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1473년 영국 데폰에서 91세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.about 1384–about 1473: tany ho any amin'ny 1384–tany ho any amin'ny 1473

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Cole I, Knight of Agincourt nasceu aproximadamente 1384, em Brixham, Torbay, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Cole Esq., tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Margarete Beaupell, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Agnes FitzWarin aproximadamente 1408, em Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1473, em Devon, Inglaterra, com 91 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Cole I, Knight of Agincourt родился около 1384 г. в местности бриксхам, торбеи, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени William Cole Esq. было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Margarete Beaupell, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Agnes FitzWarin около 1408 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1473 г. в местности девон, Англия в возрасте 91 год.

sm.about 1384–about 1473: e tusa o le 1384–e tusa o le 1473

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Cole I, Knight of Agincourt nació aproximadamente 1384, en Brixham, Torbay, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, William Cole Esq., tenía 30 años y su mamá, Margarete Beaupell, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Lady Agnes FitzWarin aproximadamente 1408, en Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1473, en Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 91 años.
